Question: Given that $$y_{p} ( x ) = -2$$ is a particular solution to $$y^{\second} - 3 y^{\prime} - 4 y = 8 ,$$ write the general solution and verify that the general solution satisfies the equation.
Find the general solution to $$y^{\second} - 4 y^{\prime} + 4 y = 7 \text{sin} t - \text{cos} t \text{.}$$
Answer: $$y ( t ) = c_{1} e^{2 t} + c_{2} t e^{2 t} + \text{sin} t + \text{cos} t$$

Question: Find the general solution to the following differential equations. $$y^{\second} - 5 y^{\prime} + 4 y = 3 e^{x}$$ $$y^{\second} + y^{\prime} - 6 y = 52 \text{cos} 2 t$$
Use Cramer’s rule to solve the following system of equations. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}2 x z_{1} - 3 z_{2} & = & 0 \\ x^{2} z_{1} + 4 x z_{2} & = & x + 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Find the general solution to the following differential equations. $$y^{\second} + y = \text{sec} x$$ $$x^{\second} - 2 x^{\prime} + x = \frac{e^{t}}{t}$$
Answer: $$y ( x ) = c_{1} \text{cos} x + c_{2} \text{sin} x + \text{cos} x \text{ln} | \text{cos} x | + x \text{sin} x$$ $$x ( t ) = c_{1} e^{t} + c_{2} t e^{t} + t e^{t} \text{ln} | t |$$
